figures.caption: Figure 1: Schematic of uniform flow past a rotating cylinder with surface speed, U∗ s .

Figure 2: Spanwise vorticity contours for flow past a rotating cylinder with U∗ s = 10U∞ and (a)-(c)Re∞ = 1000, (d)-(f)Re∞ = 2000, (g)-(i) Re∞ = 3000. The onset of the instability is shown in frames (a),(d),(g).

Figure 3: Spanwise vorticity contours for flow past a rotating cylinder with U∗ s = 10U∞ and (a)-(c)Re∞ = 4000, (d)-(f)Re∞ = 5000, (g)-(i) Re∞ = 6000. The onset of the instability is shown in frames (a),(d),(g).
Figure 4: Time variation of lift coefficient for (a) Re∞ = 1000, (c) Re∞ = 2000, (e) Re∞ = 3000 and their corresponding spectra are shown in frames (b), (d), and (f).
Figure 5: Time variation of lift coefficient for (a) Re∞ = 4000, (c) Re∞ = 5000, (e) Re∞ = 6000 and their corresponding spectra are shown in frames (b), (d), and (f).
Table 1: Dominant frequencies and their amplitudes, marked as P1, P2, P3 in Figs. 4 and 5
Figure 6: Time variation of drag coefficient for (a) Re∞ = 1000, (c) Re∞ = 2000, (e) Re∞ = 3000 and their corresponding spectra are shown in frames (b), (d), and (f).
Figure 7: Time variation of drag coefficient for (a) Re∞ = 4000, (c) Re∞ = 5000, (e) Re∞ = 6000 and their corresponding spectra are shown in frames (b), (d), and (f).
Table 2: Dominant frequencies and their amplitudes, marked as P1, P2, P3 in Figs. 6 and 7
Figure 8: Variation of (a) maximum Cl, (b) maximum Cd, and (c) onset time of instability, with Re∞, along with polynomial curve fits of different degrees.
Table 4: Polynomial regression applied to onset time, maximum Cl and maximum Cd as a function of Re∞.
Figure 9: Comparison of simulated variation of (a) maximum Cl, (b) maximum Cd, and (c) onset time of instability, as a function of Re∞ with Bayesian regression predictions using cubic-fitted curves.
Table 5: Performance of various Bayesian regression models

Figure 10: Comparison of simulated variation of (a) maximum Cl, (b) maximum Cd, and (c) onset time of instability, as a function of Re∞ with Bayesian regression predictions using spline-fitted curves.

Figure 11: Comparison of simulated variation of (a) maximum Cl, (b) maximum Cd, and (c) onset time of instability, as a function of Re∞ with Bayesian regression predictions using Gaussian-fitted curves.
Table 6: Performance of the ANN trained with maximum Cd vs Re∞ distribution.
Table 7: Performance of the ANN trained with maximum Cl vs Re∞ distribution.
Table 8: Performance of the ANN trained with onset time vs Re∞ distribution.

Figure 12: Parity plot of predicted value from ANN and actual value from simulation for (a)-(c) 85% training data, and (d)-(f) 82% training data. The maximum Cd, maximum Cl, and onset time are shown in frames (a), (d); (b), (e); and (c),(f), respectively.

Figure 13: Parity plot of predicted value from ANN and actual value from simulation for (a)-(c) 90% training data, and (d)-(f) 87% training data. The maximum Cd, maximum Cl, and onset time are shown in frames (a), (d); (b), (e); and (c),(f), respectively.
Table 9: Comparison of simulated and predicted values of maximum Cd versus Re∞ for refinement level-1.
Table 10: Comparison of simulated and predicted values of maximum Cd versus Re∞ for refinement level-2.
Figure 14: Comparison of simulated maximum Cd vs Re∞ with the predicted values from the generative ANN for (a) level-1 refinement and (b) level-2 refinement.
